# FP-43553: [Competitions] Future tournaments carry pre-release ConfigJson generated by old binary

## Status
In progress (created 2026-04-29). Steam prod was hot-patched same day: 7 future Competition tournaments were backfilled in place via `JSON_MODIFY`, 15 templates with RFC-invalid `FinishPoint` JSON were normalised, template 181 and tournament 320415 ‚Äî `Place 7` set to `Rating = 13`. A second-round defect surfaced later the same day when 318868/318869/318870 reached `StartDate`: the `Grouping` copy stripped the auto-computed `Brackets[i].MaxRating`, so `ProcessGrouping` admitted only `CompetitionRating == 0` participants into bracket 1 and left the rest unassigned. Mitigated via `MaxRating` backfill for the two still-upcoming competitions (318871, 318872); the three already-running ones left as-is (`RatingMultiplier = 1.0` makes the per-bracket math equivalent). Remaining work: replicate the diagnostic and fix on PS / XB / MOB / NX prod, extend `R202604-...sql` with `MaxRating` checks, land a code-side `InitializeGrouping` call so the runtime tolerates raw-template `Grouping` copies, plus follow-up threads on the release procedure, `JsonVerificator.exe` strictness, periodic content audit, and the deeper tech-debt refactor of the tournament generation pipeline.

## Summary
Post-deploy of the Leaderboards/Rating/Matchmaking release on Steam revealed 7 already-scheduled Competition tournaments (`Tournaments.KindId = 3`) with `ConfigJson` lacking the new fields `Places[i].Rating`, `NoShowRatingPenalty`, `ZeroScoreRatingPenalty`, and `Grouping`. Root cause: `ScheduleCompetitions` / `RandomizeCompetitions` (`Shared/SharedLib/Tournaments/TournamentSchedulingAdapter.cs`) generate each Tournament by deserialising the source template's `ConfigJson` into the C# `TournamentJsonConfig` model and re-serialising it; properties absent from the model at generation time are silently dropped, so any Tournament generated by the previous binary stays frozen on the old field set. `RegenerateFutureCompetitions` cannot heal them once registrations exist (fixed 2-hour `RegistrationStart` cutoff), and `RegenerateCompetitionsRandom` would cascade away participant data ‚Äî neither standard flow was acceptable.

While diagnosing, two adjacent issues surfaced in templates: (B) `Place 7` of template 181 had no `Rating` (a content defect that any tournament generated from the template inherits); (C) 15 active templates carry a `FinishPoint` block written as a JavaScript object literal with unquoted X/Y/Z property names ‚Äî accepted by Newtonsoft (lenient by default) but rejected by SQL Server's strict JSON functions, breaking diagnostic and patch tooling.

Diagnostic SQL captured in `SQL/Releases/R202604-Leaderboards-Rating-Matchmaking.sql` (Q1..Q5). Incident details mirrored in `modules/matchmaking/log.md` entry 2026-04-29.

## Plan
No formal phasing ‚Äî work driven by issues as they surface. Open threads (also tracked granularly in [backlog](backlog.md)):

> Audit trail of the Steam prod hot-patch ‚Äî concrete TournamentIds, source-template values, exact SQL blocks (ratings backfill, penalty backfill, `Grouping` copy, template-181 / tournament-320415 Place-7 fix, 15-template FinishPoint normalisation, second-round `Brackets[i].MaxRating` backfill), and post-patch verification: [artifacts/steam-prod-fix-2026-04-29.md](artifacts/steam-prod-fix-2026-04-29.md).

- Verify and patch the other prod streams (PS / XB / MOB / NX) using the same diagnostic SQL
- Release-procedure update: `TournamentJsonConfig` extensions must include a backfill step for already-generated future tournaments that cannot go through `RegenerateFutureCompetitions`
- `JsonVerificator.exe` strictness: add parallel RFC-strict parse (`System.Text.Json.JsonDocument.Parse`) to reject content like the unquoted-key `FinishPoint`. Sequenced *after* all platforms' templates are normalised, otherwise content—â–∏–∫–∏ lose ability to edit existing templates
- Content audit on each platform: Q3 from `R202604-...sql` for missing per-place `Rating`, Q5 for RFC-invalid templates; fix in WebAdmin
- Tech-debt: refactor `ScheduleCompetitions` / `RandomizeCompetitions` away from the deserialize‚Üíreserialize cycle. Use `JObject` for in-place patching so unknown fields survive model extensions, or add `MissingMemberHandling.Error` to make schema drift loud at generation time
- Extend `R202604-Leaderboards-Rating-Matchmaking.sql` to flag the `Grouping.Brackets[i].MaxRating` runtime-only-field gap: Q1 += `MissingBracketMaxRating` column (counted only when `Grouping` is present, to avoid double-reporting against `MissingGrouping`); Q2 += `MaxRatingVerdict` returning `'runtime'` when at least one bracket lacks `MaxRating`; header / "expected interpretation" extended to describe the third defect class. Diff drafted in section "Open threads" of the audit trail
- Code-side defensive fix: invoke `MatchmakingLogic.InitializeGrouping` in `MatchmakingLogic.ProcessGroupingForTournament` (local scope), or symmetrically in `TournamentsHelper.FromDto(TournamentDto)` (closes the gap for any future caller, mirrors the existing `FromDto(TournamentTemplateDto)` overload). Either option turns the "MaxRating must be persisted in `ConfigJson`" implicit contract into a self-healing one ‚Äî once landed cross-platform, future template-`Grouping` JSON_MODIFY backfills no longer need a parallel `MaxRating` step

## Milestones
- 2026-04-29: Diagnosis on Steam prod ‚Äî phase summary surfaced 7 affected future Competition tournaments (1 live, 6 registration-open), all with `Places[i].Rating`/`NoShowRatingPenalty`/`ZeroScoreRatingPenalty`/`Grouping` missing. 268 distinct registered users would have received `0` rating delta and skipped bracket assignment. `RegenerateFutureCompetitions` ruled out ‚Äî `RegistrationStart` already in the past for all seven. Root cause traced to deserialize‚Üíreserialize cycle in `TournamentSchedulingAdapter`
- 2026-04-29: Steam prod fix ‚Äî backfilled `Places[i].Rating` (places 1..10) and root-level `NoShowRatingPenalty` / `ZeroScoreRatingPenalty` for all 7 tournaments via point `JSON_MODIFY`, values copied from each Tournament's source template
- 2026-04-29: Steam prod fix ‚Äî backfilled `Grouping` for the 6 upcoming tournaments. Live tournament left as-is: `ProcessGrouping` had already run (no `BracketId` to assign retroactively) and `RatingMultiplier = 1.0` in the source template makes rating math identical with or without `Grouping` in the live config
- 2026-04-29: Surfaced content class C ‚Äî 15 active competition templates have RFC-invalid `FinishPoint` JSON (unquoted X/Y/Z in `Position` / `Rotation` / `Scale`). Investigated WebAdmin: `JsonVerificator.exe` and `DataEditingModels.DeserializeJson` both rely on `JsonConvert.DeserializeObject` (Newtonsoft default lenient), which accepts unquoted property names. Server-side generation also goes through Newtonsoft and silently normalises on serialise, so `Tournaments.ConfigJson` is RFC-valid even when the source template is not. SQL Server `ISJSON` / `OPENJSON` are strict and reject template directly. Normalised the 15 templates via idempotent `REPLACE` (`{ X:` ‚Üí `{ "X":`, `, Y:` ‚Üí `, "Y":`, `, Z:` ‚Üí `, "Z":`)
- 2026-04-29: Surfaced content class B ‚Äî template 181 ("Triple the Trout!") had `Place 7` with no `Rating` (other places: 35 / 30 / 25 / 22 / 19 / 15 / ? / 11 / 7 / 3). Design provided `Rating = 13`. Backfilled in template 181 and in the only in-horizon Tournament generated from it (`320415`, scheduled 2026-05-06)
- 2026-04-29: Created diagnostic SQL `SQL/Releases/R202604-Leaderboards-Rating-Matchmaking.sql` for repeated use across platforms. Five queries: phase summary (Q1), per-tournament detail with `arch`/`content`/`mixed`/`tpl-invalid` verdicts (Q2), template-side content defects on rating fields (Q3), root-level key diff Templates ‚Üî Tournaments with whitelist of expected deviations (Q4), templates/tournaments with malformed JSON (Q5). Hardened against malformed-JSON rows via `ISJSON = 1` filters and `CASE`-wrapped `OPENJSON`. All five queries verified on Steam prod returning clean state
- 2026-04-29: Recorded incident root-cause analysis in `modules/matchmaking/log.md` entry 2026-04-29 ‚Äî both the architectural class and the lesson about release procedure for `TournamentJsonConfig` extensions
- 2026-04-29: Filed JIRA FP-43553 (Bug, High, Severity major, Platform Steam, Scrum Team Leaderboards, Fix Version `2026.3.1. Leaderboards hotfix`); JIRA auto-linked it under parent Epic FP-41583 (this Epic is "Prod Bugs_2026 (All Platforms)" ‚Äî the common 2026 prod-bugs umbrella across all platforms, not a release-specific Epic). KB journal created (this file)
- 2026-04-29: Compiled audit trail of concrete IDs / values / transactional SQL blocks in [artifacts/steam-prod-fix-2026-04-29.md](artifacts/steam-prod-fix-2026-04-29.md). Same patterns are applicable verbatim to PS / XB / MOB / NX, but the values must be re-derived from each platform's own `TournamentTemplates`
- 2026-04-29 (evening): Diagnosis ‚Äî matchmaking on 318868 (ended), 318869 (ended), 318870 (live) reported only 35‚Äì45 % of registered participants were assigned to a single group A in bracket 1, the rest left with `BracketId IS NULL`, `GroupName IS NULL`. Traced to section A.4 of the audit trail: `JSON_QUERY(tt.ConfigJson, '$.Grouping')` returns the template's `Grouping` without `Brackets[i].MaxRating` (templates never store it ‚Äî auto-computed by `MatchmakingLogic.InitializeGrouping` during template deserialization), and `TournamentsHelper.FromDto(TournamentDto)` does not invoke that initializer at competition start. With `int MaxRating` defaulting to 0, `MatchmakingLogic.CreateBuckets` admitted only `CompetitionRating == 0` (incl. `NULL` mapped to 0) into bucket 1 and produced inverted-empty ranges `[101..0]` / `[1001..0]` for buckets 2 and 3. The regular generation pipeline (`ScheduleCompetitions` / `RandomizeCompetitions`) sidesteps the asymmetry by deserializing the template first and re-serializing the now-initialized `Grouping` into `Tournaments.ConfigJson`, so all `TournamentId >= 320342` rows are healthy by construction. Confluence Matchmaking spec (page 5505613835) confirms `MaxRating` is auto-computed and "do not set manually" ‚Äî formalises the implicit contract this defect class breaks
- 2026-04-29 (evening): Steam prod fix ‚Äî backfilled `Grouping.Brackets[i].MaxRating` (100 / 1000 / 2147483647 ‚Äî recomputed from each bracket's `MinRating[i+1] - 1` rule, matches what `InitializeGrouping` would compute) for the two still-upcoming Competition tournaments 318871 and 318872 via point `JSON_MODIFY`, applied >5 minutes before each `StartDate` so the AsyncProcessor `GetTournamentsToStartSoon` re-poll picks up the patched ConfigJson before `ProcessGrouping` runs (no Tournament-DTO-level cache exists; `TournamentsCache` only covers `TournamentSeries`). The three already-running tournaments (318868 ended, 318869 ended, 318870 live) left as-is ‚Äî `ProcessGrouping` had already executed and `RatingMultiplier = 1.0` on every bracket makes per-bracket rating math equivalent. `MaxRating`-aware phase summary post-patch: 138 far-future + 1 reg-opens-soon + 6 reg-open + 1 live; only the deliberate 318870 row reports `GroupingPresentButMaxMissing > 0`. Audit trail extended with section D in `artifacts/steam-prod-fix-2026-04-29.md`, plus an "Open threads" block listing the diagnostic-script extension, the code-side defensive fix candidates, and the cross-platform replication notes
